cmd.planet_icons_tooltip: Show orbiting status icons around planets in this system

ship.custom_3d: I WANT MY OWN SHIP DESIGN
ship.custom_hint: We will craft a unique ship only for you
ship.custom_ready_desc: Your personal ship is in the hangar. If the model cannot load in the arena, the system will launch the blue reserve chassis automatically.

ship_design.title: Unique ship
ship_design.body: Describe the ship you want to receive. We will craft a personal 3D hull for your hangar and arena. Quarks are charged only after the description passes review.
ship_design.hints: Mention hull shape, purpose, colors, nose character, wings or engines. Avoid brands, characters, personal data and adult content.
ship_design.placeholder: Example: a compact scout ship with a wedge-shaped nose, dark blue hull, copper lines, two side engines and soft green lights...
ship_design.no_quarks: Not enough quarks to craft the ship.
ship_design.status_ready: Ship is ready to add to the hangar.
ship_design.status_failed: Could not craft the ship. Try again.
ship_design.status_timeout: Crafting is taking longer than usual. Check the hangar later.

raid.title: Carrier Raid
raid.controls: WASD to fly, mouse turns nose, LMB laser, RMB missile, Space warp
raid.exit_body: Progress in this combat operation will be lost.

planet.photo_tooltip: Rent supertélescope capacity to capture a unique image of the selected planet. The image will belong exclusively to you.
planet.photo_exosphere_tooltip: Kling capture from an orbital probe: the planet, its moons, and the system star in a realistic mission frame.

colony_hub.name: Colony Hub
colony_hub.desc: Main base. Built-in observatory (+1 data/hr), 500 residents, +5 energy.

resource_storage.name: Resource Module
resource_storage.desc: Colony storage module. Expands resource capacity by +5000.

landing_pad.name: Landing Pad
landing_pad.desc: Launch and reception pad for small exploration craft: probes, satellites, rovers, and drones.

spaceport.name: Spaceport
spaceport.desc: Heavy orbital logistics: cargo ships, colonization launches, and terraforming resource delivery. Requires Landing Pad.

solar_plant.name: Solar Plant
solar_plant.desc: Energy from the star. Efficiency depends on orbital distance.

battery_station.name: Battery Station
battery_station.desc: Stores surplus energy. +2400 energy storage capacity for covering consumption spikes.

wind_generator.name: Wind Generator
wind_generator.desc: Wind energy. Requires atmosphere. Output depends on pressure.

thermal_generator.name: Thermal Generator
thermal_generator.desc: Stable energy from geothermal sources. Bonus on volcanoes.

mine.name: Mine
mine.desc: Passive mineral extraction from surface deposits. Consumes energy and a little water.

water_extractor.name: Water Extractor
water_extractor.desc: Extracts water from ocean, ice, or coastline. Consumes energy.

atmo_extractor.name: Atmospheric Condenser
atmo_extractor.desc: Condenses volatiles from atmosphere. Consumes energy; output depends on pressure.

deep_drill.name: Deep Drill
deep_drill.desc: Accesses deep deposits. Strong passive mining and a chance for rare elements.

orbital_collector.name: Orbital Collector
orbital_collector.desc: Sole extraction method for gas/ice giants.

isotope_collector.name: Isotope Collector
isotope_collector.desc: Passive isotope extraction from cosmic radiation. 15 ISO/hr.

observatory.name: Observatory
observatory.desc: Space observation. +5 research data/hr. Research session slots.

radar_tower.name: Radar Tower
radar_tower.desc: Planetary sensor array. Refines deposit, atmosphere-risk, and mission telemetry.

greenhouse.name: Greenhouse
greenhouse.desc: Closed food module. Provides food support for population growth, but does not change planet habitability.

residential_dome.name: Residential Dome
residential_dome.desc: Crew housing. Adds population space, but growth is blocked without enough food support.

atmo_shield.name: Atmospheric Shield
atmo_shield.desc: Protects the colony from hostile atmosphere, dust storms, and micrometeorites. Does not terraform the planet.

biome_dome.name: Biome Dome
biome_dome.desc: Self-contained biocircuit. Provides large food support and stable life support without directly raising habitability.

quantum_separator.name: Quantum Separator
quantum_separator.desc: L50 elemental separation lab. Converts minerals into base elements for late chemistry.

gas_fractionator.name: Gas Fractionator
gas_fractionator.desc: L50 volatile fractionator. Extracts H/He/N/C/S for biosphere, reactors, and synthesis.

isotope_centrifuge.name: Isotope Centrifuge
isotope_centrifuge.desc: L50 isotope enrichment. Produces U/Th for fusion, Genesis Vault, and endgame construction.

genesis_vault.name: Genesis Vault
genesis_vault.desc: DNA archive and terraforming control center. Unlocks global planet-changing processes.

alpha_harvester.name: Alpha Harvester
alpha_harvester.desc: Premium drone. Auto-harvests available resources within 2 zones around itself. Purchased with quarks.

phy-thrust-1.name: Thrust I
phy-thrust-1.desc: Unlocks the Landing Pad for small exploration launches.

chem-orbital.name: Orbital Collection
chem-orbital.desc: Unlocks Orbital Collector for gas giants.

errors.surfaceChaosBody: The planet is in chaos — now is not the time for this. Find a new home.
errors.appleCredential: Apple authorization completed, but Firebase rejected the credential. Check Apple Sign-In settings for bundle id app.nebulife.game.

research.signal_weak: Commander, deep scan of system {{system}} detected an anomaly: {{name}}. Signal is weak, requesting telescope resource allocation.
research.insufficient_data: Insufficient research data. Collect more data by exploring the galaxy.
research.regen_hint: Data regenerates automatically (+1/hr) and from surface buildings.

surface.title: Surface
surface.rover_mode_hint: click on the map to scout
surface.demolish_warning_body: Are you sure you want to demolish this structure? The process is irreversible, resources will not be returned.
surface.build_place_hint: Click on the map to place the building

quest.step0_desc: Open the build panel (hammer icon at the bottom) and select 'Colony Hub'. Place it on an open area.
quest.step1_desc: Your scout drone is active! It flies across the map and reveals the fog of war. It uses isotopes (you have 100 at start). Without isotopes the drone will stop.
quest.step2_desc: Collect surface resources: trees and ore yield minerals, thermal vents yield isotopes. Tap a resource to collect.
quest.step3_desc: The drone reveals new zones as it moves. Build a Solar Plant — with it the drone flies for free, no isotopes consumed!
quest.step4_desc: Build a Solar Plant to generate energy. Without energy, buildings stop working!

discovery.notification: Observatory detected signal: {{name}} in system {{system}}. Awaiting operator decision.

observatory.title: Nebulife Observatory

duration_desc.1h: {{chance}}% chance to find a standard, anomalous, or rare event.
duration_desc.6h: {{chance}}% chance to find a rare signal; epic echoes are possible.
duration_desc.24h: {{chance}}% chance for an epic or legendary signature. If none is found, the observatory guarantees a standard event.

observatory.search_explainer: Space research searches for unique events and objects for the catalog. Level chance: {{chance}}% at this observatory level; levels 1-5 give 10%, 20%, 30%, 35%, and 40%. Longer searches change the target rarity, and 24-hour searches never return empty.
observatory.search_started: Observatory search started. Results will arrive when the session completes.
observatory.search_no_signal_log: Observatory search completed: no stable cosmic signal detected.
observatory.search_found_log: Observatory detected a new cosmic event: {{name}}.
observatory.search_duplicate_log: Observatory detected a repeated cosmic event: {{name}}.
observatory.level_up_log: Observatory upgraded to level {{level}}. New search programs and signal filters are available.

program_desc.routine_sky_watch: Level 1. Good for short 1-hour searches for standard, anomalous, and rare signals.
program_desc.anomaly_sweep: Level 2. Filters background noise better and pulls anomalous signatures more often.
program_desc.phenomenon_survey: Level 3. Tunes sensors for rare phenomena and weak epic echoes.
program_desc.deep_space_watch: Level 4. Opens deep searches for epic and legendary signatures.
program_desc.catalog_completion: Level 5. Biases selection more strongly toward catalog entries you have not found yet.

player.web_only_topup: Top-up available via web version only
player.create_account_hint: Save your progress permanently. Sign in with Google or email.
player.reset_warning: This action cannot be undone. All progress will be lost.
player.delete_warning: All data will be deleted with no way to recover.
player.level_up_nodes_unlocked: New nodes unlocked in the Research Terminal

settings.delete_confirm_text: This will permanently delete all your data. Type DELETE to confirm.

auth.subtitle: exploring the universe
auth.legacy_progress: You have saved progress. Sign in to keep it.
auth.guest_note: Guest mode: progress is saved, quark purchases unavailable
auth.has_account_login: Already have an account? Sign In

radial.alpha_hint: Get a photo from the Alpha telescope. Uses quarks to rent a cutting-edge telescope.

context_menu.panorama_tooltip: Rent supertélescope capacity to capture a unique panorama of the selected star system. The image will belong exclusively to you.

system_info.tip_class: Spectral class: determines the color, temperature and type of the star
system_info.tip_temperature: Surface temperature in Kelvin (K). Sun ≈ 5778 K
system_info.tip_mass: Mass relative to the Sun. M☉ = 1.99×10³⁰ kg
system_info.tip_luminosity: Radiated power relative to the Sun. L☉ ≈ 3.83×10²⁶ W
system_info.tip_age: Star age in billions of years. Gyr = 10⁹ years

planet_terminal.logistics_desc: Resource delivery to this planet through free cargo ships from spaceports.
planet_terminal.no_cargo_ships: No free cargo ships. Build transport at a spaceport.
planet_terminal.terraform_preview_l48: Terraforming requirements are visible now. Launch unlocks at L48.

planet_missions.no_reports: No reports for this planet yet.
planet_missions.resources_locked: Resources are hidden. Requires an orbital probe (Tier 2).
planet_missions.elements_locked: Element details unlock after a surface expedition (Tier 3).

reason.already_revealed: this data tier is already revealed
reason.active_mission: a mission is already active for this planet

production.queue_full: production queue for this payload is full

report_body.header: {{type}} for {{planet}} is complete. Preliminary science brief for the Colony Council follows.
report_body.footer: Recommendation: use this data for the next mission tier or colonization planning.

arena.exit_body: Are you sure you want to leave the combat zone?

surface_panel.select_cell: select a cell on the map
surface_panel.no_buildings_line2: Select a type in the BUILDINGS tab.

evacuation.forced_message: Asteroid on final trajectory. We did not find an ideal planet, but there is no choice. The evacuation ship is ready. We fly and hope this world becomes our new home.

colony.ship_in_orbit: The ship has entered stable orbit around planet {{planet}}. All systems ready for landing.

guest.reminder_prefix: Dear President, to save your space exploration progress —
guest.error_apple_in_use: This Apple account is already in use
guest.error_google_in_use: This Google account is already in use

link_account.title: Link Account
link_account.hint: Link your account to purchase quarks and sync progress across devices
link_account.error_apple_in_use: This Apple account is already in use
link_account.error_google_in_use: This Google account is already in use

callsign.title: Choose Callsign
callsign.hint: Your unique name in the Nebulife universe
callsign.invalid_format: Callsign must be 3-20 characters (Latin letters, digits, _ or -)

topup.title: Top Up Quarks
topup.iap_note: Purchases confirmed via App Store / Google Play
topup.ads_failed: Ad not available. Try again later.

resource_display.research_data_title: Research data — resource for scanning systems

desc.observatories_desc: Active research stations in stellar orbits. Each observatory scans a star system, collects spectral data, and detects anomalies.
desc.research_data_desc: Accumulated results of telescopic observation and spectral analysis. Consumed when launching research of new star systems.
desc.research_data_elements: Electromagnetic spectrum, radio waves, gravitational mapping, neutrino flux
desc.minerals_desc: Solid rock and metals extracted from the planet's crust. The primary building resource for colonial structures.
desc.volatiles_desc: Liquids and gases from the surface and atmosphere. Used as fuel, chemical feedstock, and for life support systems.
desc.isotopes_desc: Radioactive and rare isotopes from the planet's mantle and core. The primary energy source for reactors and warp drives.
desc.quarks_desc: The in-game currency unit. Used to expand the fleet, generate AI images, and unlock premium features.
desc.water_desc: Liquid water extracted from surface deposits and underground reservoirs. Essential for crew sustenance, food production, and chemical processes in colony domes.

archive.title: Control Center
archive.resources_periodic_locked: Detailed resource breakdown into periodic-table elements unlocks at level {{level}}. For now, only total colony stockpiles are available.
archive.no_mission_resources: No resources available for missions to other planets
archive.no_elements_owned: No elements from this group have been separated yet
archive.tooltip_planet_count_system: Number of planets in the system
archive.ring_locked_hint: research all systems in the previous ring
archive.instant_research_desc: For {{cost}} ⚛ you can research this system in 1 click
archive.no_favorites_hint: Click on a planet in the Planets section and select Add to Favorites

cinematic.goal_find: > Find a planet suitable for colonization
cinematic.goal_launch: > Launch the Rescue Ship with 10,000 passengers
cinematic.fate_line: The fate of your people is in your hands.
cinematic.time_limit: You have 1 day. Every minute counts.
cinematic.subtitle_1: Millions of commanders in a shared universe.
cinematic.subtitle_2: The Nebulife galaxy -- just a grain of sand on the map of existence.
cinematic.subtitle_3: Are you ready to conquer the universe?
cinematic.civilization_intro: Your civilization has existed for thousands of years among the stars. But time is running out.

gallery.use_supertelescope: Use the Supertelescope in the star system or planet menu.

photo.replace_question: The archive already has a photo of this object. Replace?

tutorial.step_awakening_text: Commander, connection established. I am A.S.T.R.A., your adaptive biobot for navigation and scientific analysis. Your home world is in danger. We have limited time to find a new habitable planet.
tutorial.step_terminal_text: This is the Command Center. Here you will track resources, research data, systems, colonies and every signal we receive from the galaxy. Open the Terminal, and I will guide the first scan.
tutorial.step_nav_planets_text: All information about your mission is gathered here. Navigate to the Planets tab.
tutorial.step_expand_star_text: Expand your home star to see the planets in the system.
tutorial.step_add_fav_text: Add your home planet to Favorites for quick access.
tutorial.step_check_fav_text: Check the Favorites tab — your planet is now there.
tutorial.step_go_systems_text: These are the nearest star systems. Each one may hide planets, resources or anomalies. Choose an available system for the first scan. We are looking for a chance to survive.
tutorial.step_first_research_text: The observatory is starting to collect data. At first we only see weak signals: star class, possible planets and rough conditions. The higher the research progress, the clearer the picture becomes.
tutorial.step_hud_obs_text: Data is coming in. This progress is not just a bar; it is our vision in the dark. Every percent can reveal temperature, water, or a possible path to a new home.
tutorial.step_hud_data_text: Research data powers every scan. Later it will also help reveal surface deposits, building sites and planetary mission details.
tutorial.step_free_task_text: Launch 2 more scans. Parallel observatories are your main exploration pace during the first hour.
tutorial.step_anomaly_text: Signal confirmed. This system contains an object worth a closer look. If the data is stable enough, I can prepare a visual reconstruction of your first discovery.
tutorial.step_quantum_text: Choose Quantum Focus for detailed analysis. This will turn raw signals into a clear visual artifact. Your first discovery analysis is free.
tutorial.step_save_gallery_text: There it is: a world or phenomenon no one in this cluster has seen before you. Save the image to your Gallery so it becomes part of your exploration history.
tutorial.step_gallery_final_text: The Gallery is your archive of discoveries: planets, anomalies, life forms and future expeditions. Over time it will become your personal atlas of the galaxy.
tutorial.step_astra_handoff_text: The first protocol is complete. I will remain in your communication channel from now on: explaining events, helping with decisions and answering questions about the galaxy.

log.empty_all: Log is empty. Events will appear here.

chat.title: Chat
chat.blocked: You are temporarily blocked from chat.
chat.astra_online: A.S.T.R.A. online. Ask a question, Commander.

premium.title: Premium
premium.no_ads: No ads · 50 daily AI chats · +5 Quarks daily

mission_report.procedural_photo_desc: Black-and-white instrument photo generated from mission data.
mission_report.alpha_photo_desc: Photorealistic Gemini/Nano Banana generation grounded in this report.

event.msg1: Commander, asteroid trajectory calculations have been updated. Time is critically short...
event.msg2: But there is good news. It seems this system has what we have been searching for.
event.trajectory_body: Commander, asteroid trajectory calculations have been updated. Time is critically short. But there is good news — it seems this system has what we have been searching for. We must hurry!

perf.title: GRAPHICS
perf.subtitle: Choose the mode that fits your device

simple.title: SIMPLE
simple.desc: For budget phones and older tablets. No clouds, simplified starfield, no rotation.

standard.title: STANDARD
standard.desc: For most devices. Balanced look and smoothness.

full.title: FULL
full.desc: For flagship devices (Samsung S22+, iPhone 14 Pro+). All effects, full detail.

perf.change_later_hint: Can be changed later in Settings

mission.title: Why Nebulife Exists
mission.intro: A short commander doctrine: what you are doing in the galaxy, why every scan matters, and how the game turns exploration into understanding.

surface.title: First Lesson on the Surface
surface.summary: A.S.T.R.A. explains what you have really started building.
surface.body_1: Commander, landing is complete. This is not merely the surface of a new planet. It is the first point of your civilization in a game built by one person for millions of adult space enthusiasts across the world.
surface.body_2: Nebulife is made for players who want more than fast rewards and empty levels. Here curiosity matters more than noise: stars have physics, planets have chemistry, colonies have economy, and every discovery can become part of your personal history.
surface.body_3: You are not only surviving after a catastrophe. You are entering a shared, expanding universe with millions of planets and stars, where late game will open player interaction, trade, alliances, expansion, and the creation of new unique life on your worlds.
surface.body_4: Feel like a creator: not a god above a finished backdrop, but an intelligent participant in a living system where your colony, discoveries, and decisions gradually become part of a larger cosmos.
surface.meaning_1: The surface begins the colony economy: resources, energy, buildings, and survival.
surface.meaning_2: Academy explains not only astronomy, but the meaning of what you are building.
surface.meaning_3: Late game is designed as a shared space for adult players: trade, alliances, expansion, and biological creation.
surface.action: Look around the first hexes near the colony center. This is not a starter area; it is the first cell of your future universe.

why.title: Why This Matters
why.summary: Nebulife turns curiosity into a long cosmic journey.
why.body_1: Nebulife begins as a game about civilization survival, but its real foundation is broader: giving adult players the feeling of real discovery again.
why.body_2: Each star, planet, signal, and image is not decoration. It is part of the language the universe uses to explain temperature, water, orbits, chemistry, life, and risk.
why.body_3: Your role is not to tap everything at random. You are a commander and explorer who gradually turns the unknown into a map of decisions.
why.meaning_1: Level growth comes from exploration, not random rewards.
why.meaning_2: Academy explains the real principles behind the game data.
why.meaning_3: The Archive stores not trophies for their own sake, but a history of what you have understood.
why.action: Open any researched object in the Archive and ask yourself: what exactly made it worth studying?

mission.title: Your Mission
mission.summary: The planet is doomed, but civilization still has a chance.
mission.body_1: Your home system, Alpha Lyrae, gave your civilization thousands of years among the stars. Now an asteroid threat forces action: find a new world and prepare evacuation.
mission.body_2: The seven-day story timer is not just pressure. It is a frame that turns knowledge into decisions: which systems to scan, which planet to verify, when to risk a mission.
mission.body_3: Nebulife asks a simple question: can a civilization survive if it learns to read data faster than disaster arrives?
mission.meaning_1: Observatories open paths toward potentially habitable worlds.
mission.meaning_2: Planet missions reduce uncertainty before colonization.
mission.meaning_3: Evacuation is the result of research, not a separate button.
mission.action: Look at the nearest system and decide which data you still need before choosing a colony target.

method.title: The Research Method
method.body_1: Science in Nebulife is presented as a gameplay loop. First the signal is vague, then an observatory clarifies the star, planets, temperature, water, and habitability.
method.body_2: Each new layer of data changes your confidence. What looked promising at 25% can become a trap after a mission, or become your next home.
method.body_3: Academy is not decoration. It gives you the language to understand why telemetry numbers matter.
method.meaning_1: Research Data is the fuel for reducing the unknown.
method.meaning_2: Academy quests train the same skills you need on the map.
method.meaning_3: Quizzes reinforce concepts that later appear in systems and missions.
method.action: After the next lesson, open the quest and identify the real parameter it trains: star class, water, temperature, or habitability.

livingGalaxy.title: The Living Galaxy
livingGalaxy.summary: A shared universe growing around real player decisions.
livingGalaxy.body_1: Nebulife does not draw identical levels for everyone. The galaxy is generated deterministically: the same inputs create the same cosmos.
livingGalaxy.body_2: Each cluster connects a group of players and a large number of stars. You begin locally, then discover that your system is only the doorway into a wider structure.
livingGalaxy.body_3: A living galaxy means that news, discoveries, Academy, colonies, trade, alliances, and future social systems grow together instead of being isolated screens.
livingGalaxy.meaning_1: Neighbor systems and the Core Zone create a shared frontier.
livingGalaxy.meaning_2: Determinism keeps space fair: purchases do not rewrite the universe's physics.
livingGalaxy.meaning_3: The weekly digest connects real space news back into the game.
livingGalaxy.action: Open the galaxy scale view and see how your home zone connects to the larger cluster.

artifacts.title: Discovery Artifacts
artifacts.summary: Photos, reports, and ships are evidence of your path.
artifacts.body_1: In Nebulife, a beautiful image is not just cosmetic. A planet photo, mission report, or unique ship records a moment that belongs to your playthrough.
artifacts.body_2: An artifact has emotional and educational value: it helps you remember the object, share it, and return to what was discovered.
artifacts.body_3: That is why the Gallery gradually becomes a personal atlas, not a folder of pictures.
artifacts.meaning_1: A saved photo shows that a discovery became part of your story.
artifacts.meaning_2: Mission images are tied to planet data and expedition type.
artifacts.meaning_3: Unique ships and future life-form artifacts extend the player's identity in the universe.
artifacts.action: Review the Gallery and pick one object that best explains your expedition.

future.title: Where This Is Going
future.summary: From first colony to alliances, expansion, and new life.
future.body_1: Nebulife is designed as a system that grows in layers. First you learn to scan, then choose a world, then build a colony and open more complex routes.
future.body_2: Academy should grow with that arc: today it explains stars and planets; later it helps with colony chemistry, trade, life, social systems, and the deep core.
future.body_3: The philosophy is simple: the game should become more interesting as the player becomes smarter and bolder in their own expansion.
future.meaning_1: Colonies turn knowledge about a planet into survival economy.
future.meaning_2: New lessons and digests give a reason to return each week.
future.meaning_3: Deep Core and social systems should turn discovery into a shared history.
future.action: Return to Academy after your next major discovery and ask: what new layer of the game did it unlock for me?

surface_intro.title: Commander, you are not building a base. You are starting a universe.
surface_intro.body_1: Nebulife is a unique authored game built by one person for millions of adult space enthusiasts. It is not about random tapping; it is about feeling that you are truly discovering and shaping something large.
surface_intro.body_2: The surface is where the real scale begins: resources, energy, buildings, colonies, expansion. Later will come player interaction, trade, alliances, and the creation of new unique life on your planets.
surface_intro.body_3: I prepared the first lesson to explain Nebulife's philosophy and your role inside a shared, expanding universe with millions of planets and stars.

quarks_unlock.title: UNLOCK SLOT WITH QUARKS
quarks_unlock.body: Short on resources? You can instantly unlock this slot for 10 quarks.

hint_5slots.title: Your colony wakes up
hint_5slots.body: You've unlocked your first 5 slots. Each revealed hex either drops a resource or becomes a build spot. Yields taper from the centre outward — inner slots are the most generous. Running low? Tap any locked hex to pay 10 quarks instead. Work the first ring systematically: on the home planet it's guaranteed to give you all 4 resource types.

premium.resource_desc: Increases all production on this colony. Lasts 24 hours, local only.
premium.time_desc: Reduces resource harvest cooldown on this colony. Lasts 24 hours.
premium.disclaimer: Boost is local — applies to this planet only. Other colonies have their own independent upgrades.

building_detail.action_open_center_desc: Jump to the full colony management screen.
building_detail.action_scan_desc: Spend 5 Research Data to clarify nearby deposits and signals.
building_detail.action_radar_deposit_scan_desc: Spend 5 Research Data to refine finite deposit estimates around this colony.
building_detail.action_radar_weather_desc: Check storm, dust, and atmospheric hazard telemetry for surface operations.
building_detail.action_radar_mission_sync_desc: Refresh local navigation beacons for probes, carriers, and surface expeditions.
building_detail.action_inspect_deposit_desc: Check the finite stock that feeds this extraction chain.
building_detail.action_chemistry_desc: Convert 10 volatiles into 3 isotopes and 2 water.
building_detail.action_landing_pad_production_desc: Open the Colony Center production queue for small exploration craft.
building_detail.landing_pad_dispatch_hint: Orbital probes, satellites, and surface reconnaissance are dispatched from the planet menu in System view.
building_detail.transport_landing_pad_desc: Small probes, satellites, rovers, and reusable science carriers. The hub manages the colony, it does not build transport.
building_detail.transport_spaceport_desc: Heavy logistics, cargo ships, terraforming freighters, and colony ships.

transport_help.research_shuttle: Reusable carrier for orbital survey probes and satellites. After production it is stored as a fleet ship on this planet.
transport_help.rover_dropcraft: Reusable landing transport for surface expeditions and rover deployment.
transport_help.atmo_probe_carrier: Reusable carrier for reinforced atmosphere probes used on gas and ice giants.
transport_help.survey_probe: One-shot orbital probe for revealing more detailed planet data.
transport_help.orbital_satellite: Orbital science satellite for stable observation and data relay.
transport_help.surface_rover: Surface rover for ground reconnaissance, sampling, and colonization checks.
transport_help.atmosphere_probe: Reinforced probe for descending into dense or hazardous atmospheres.
transport_help.scout_drone: Fast reconnaissance drone for local mapping and checking dangerous zones.
transport_help.lander: Landing module for delivering a field lab or platform to the surface.
transport_help.research_station_kit: Deployable kit that unfolds into a field research station on another planet.
transport_help.transport_small: Small cargo ship for routine logistics between colonies.
transport_help.transport_large: Heavy cargo ship for large batches of resources and construction materials.
transport_help.terraform_freighter: Specialized freighter for delivering resources to terraforming projects.
transport_help.colony_ship: Colony ship carrying colonists and a starter hub kit.

building_detail.action_result_stock: Scan complete. Deposit telemetry stabilized at {{pct}}% remaining.
building_detail.action_result_signal: Scan complete. No finite stock is attached to this module yet, but local telemetry is updated.
building_detail.action_result_deposit: {{resource}} source: {{remaining}} U left, {{pct}}% of initial stock.
building_detail.action_result_no_deposit: No finite deposit is connected to this building yet.
building_detail.action_result_power: Power route is stable. Critical consumers remain prioritized during shortages.
building_detail.action_result_chemistry: Cycle complete: volatiles split into isotope traces and condensed water.
building_detail.action_result_radar_weather: Forecast updated: surface routes and atmospheric risk zones are synchronized with colony sensors.
building_detail.action_result_radar_mission_sync: Mission beacons synchronized. Local probe telemetry has a clean navigation fix.
building_detail.isotope_depleted_warning: Isotope deposits on this planet are depleted. Building this extractor here is not useful right now.
building_detail.demolish_desc: The module will be removed from the surface. Construction resources are not returned.

terraform.no_free_ships: No free ships at the selected spaceport

hex.storage_full_warning: Storage is full. Extraction is stopped.

landing.title: Enter a living galaxy that keeps expanding
landing.subtitle: Nebulife is not a map with levels. It is a procedural universe with stellar physics, orbital mechanics and planetary chemistry, where every tester begins inside one cluster, then helps reveal a much larger cosmos hidden beyond the signal.
landing.hero_brief_title: Enter a living galaxy before anyone else
landing.hero_brief_body: Explore real-scale systems, generate expedition artifacts, and help tune Nebulife for Android and iOS launch.
landing.testers_title: Test Nebulife on Android and iOS
landing.testers_body: We are collecting mobile testers first: onboarding, battery, performance, exosphere quality, terminal UX, and Academy flow. Join the Telegram group to get builds, notes, and feedback calls.
landing.final_subtitle: Join the Android and iOS test group now. The browser version will open later when the mobile test is stable.

lead.label: Tester lead
lead.title: Leave your email to start testing
lead.body: We will use this contact to understand who joined the early mobile test. After email confirmation, the test group button appears here.
lead.consent: We will only use this email for Android/iOS test access and Nebulife tester updates.
lead.invalid: Enter a valid email to continue.
lead.save_error: Could not save the lead. Check connection and try again.
lead.ready_body: The mobile tester group is ready. Join it to receive Android and iOS build instructions.

universe.label: Beyond the first signal
universe.title: A cluster is only the doorway
universe.body: Each 50-player group receives a living cluster with 1,450 stars and more than 8,000 planets and moons. But that is only the local frontier. Nebulife is designed as an expanding universe: new clusters, new discoveries, scientific expedition artifacts, and unknown routes deeper into the core.
universe.fact_2_label: deterministic systems shared by your group
universe.fact_3_label: planets and moons waiting for scans, colonies, and anomalies

showcase.label: Look first
showcase.title: A site that feels like opening the game
showcase.body: Nebulife has to sell the fantasy before the install: a nebula-lit galaxy, believable planets, generated expedition images, and arena ships that already hint at motion and danger.
showcase.planet_title: Planets with scale, storms, rings, and atmosphere
showcase.planet_body: The landing page now previews the kind of worlds players will inspect: Earth-like worlds, gas giants, ice planets, atmospheric glow, and orbital drama.
showcase.photo_title: Every discovery can become a personal cosmic image
showcase.photo_body: The page shows the idea itself: photo-like mission frames that feel collectible before the player even joins testing.
showcase.ships_title: Ships, asteroids, team colors, and combat mood
showcase.ships_body: The tester sees that Nebulife is not only research: there is a hangar, ship identity, arena movement, missiles, and dangerous asteroid fields.

features.live_body: Procedural events, weekly digests, and evolving discoveries keep the universe fresh.
features.ai_body: Collect mission artifacts — exosphere imagery and field reports tied to your own planets.
features.learn_body: Academy turns sessions into actual science learning with adaptive difficulty.
features.cluster_body: Fifty testers share one cluster, but the architecture keeps opening new star fields beyond it.

flow.step_1: Start on your home planet and open your star map.
flow.step_2: Run observatory research sessions to reveal systems.
flow.step_3: Inspect exospheres and compare worlds by habitability.
flow.step_4: Launch expeditions and collect scientific discovery artifacts.
flow.step_5: Build colony infrastructure and prepare evacuation decisions.

proof.exosphere_body: Inspect planet-scale atmosphere, rings, clouds, storms, and surface signatures.
proof.galaxy_body: Move through a deterministic cluster where every star has a place and purpose.
proof.terminal_body: Plan research branches, compare discoveries, and control your expedition flow.
proof.surface_body: Build a colony from terrain, resources, energy, and survival constraints.
proof.academy_body: Learn real astronomy through adaptive missions connected to your progress.
